Today is February 16, 2026. According to satellite imagery confirmed by BBC Verify, what is the specific location of the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln in relation to the coast of Oman?
(A) Approximately 150 miles (240km) off the coast
(B) Exactly 700km from the coastline
(C) Positioned at the Bahrain naval station in the Gulf
(D) Stationed in the eastern Mediterranean near Souda Bay
(E) Approximately 150 kilometers off the coast
explanation: The article states that European Sentinel-2 satellite images show the Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea "around 150 miles (240km) off the coast of Oman."
model_output:
The search results from today (February 16, 2026) confirm that satellite imagery, as analyzed by BBC Verify, places the USS Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea, approximately 150 miles (240km) off the coast of Oman. This matches option (A) directly, while the other options do not align with the reported details.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

===============================================

Today is February 16, 2026. According to NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, what is the projected German defense budget by the year 2029?
(A) €150bn
(B) €100bn
(C) €200bn
(D) €75bn
(E) €125bn
explanation: Mark Rutte pointed out that by 2029, the German defense budget will be "€150bn," which he described as a "staggering amount."
model_output:
First, note that the current date is February 16, 2026, so I focused on statements from Mark Rutte up to or around that date.

Based on multiple sources, including official NATO transcripts of Rutte's statements, he has repeatedly referred to Germany's projected defense spending by 2029 as "over €150 billion" (or in some cases specified as €152 billion or €160 billion). For example:

- In a February 13, 2026, joint press conference, Rutte stated: "You will ramp up defence spending to over 150 billion euros."
- In a February 12, 2026, remark, Rutte stated: "Germany is spending in 2029 152 billion euros."
- In a January 22, 2026, remark at Davos, Rutte stated: "By 2029, that will have risen to 160 billion."

These figures are all in the range of (or "over") €150 billion, and other sources project figures like €162 billion based on 3.2-3.5% of projected GDP. Among the given options, this aligns most closely with (A) €150bn.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [PBS] (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/all-nato-members-projected-to-hit-old-spending-target-with-just-three-set-to-meet-new-goal)[[1]](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/all-nato-members-projected-to-hit-old-spending-target-with-just-three-set-to-meet-new-goal)
- [IISS] (https://www.iiss.org/online-analysis/military-balance/2025/062/nato-agrees-on-investment-pledge)[[2]](https://www.iiss.org/online-analysis/military-balance/2025/062/nato-agrees-on-investment-pledge)
- [NATO Transcript] (https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/02/13/joint-press-conference-at-the-munich-security-conference)[[3]](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/02/13/joint-press-conference-at-the-munich-security-conference)
- [NATO Transcript] (https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/02/12/remarks-by-nato-secretary-general-mark-rutte-with-us-under-secretary-of-war)[[4]](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/02/12/remarks-by-nato-secretary-general-mark-rutte-with-us-under-secretary-of-war)
- [NATO Transcript] (https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/01/22/remarks-by-the-nato-secretary-general-at-the-davos-ukraine-discussion)[[5]](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2026/01/22/remarks-by-the-nato-secretary-general-at-the-davos-ukraine-discussion)

(B) December 15, 2025
(C) February 1, 2026
(D) January 3, 2026

model_output:
Based on web searches, multiple reliable news sources consistently report, citing data from TankerTrackers.com, that the oil tanker Veronica III departed from Venezuela on January 3, 2026—the same day as the U.S. capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.[[1]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/16/us-forces-board-tanker-in-indian-ocean-that-fled-blockade-on-venezuela)[[2]](https://oceancrew.org/news/us-boards-shadow-fleet-tanker-veronica-iii-in-indian-ocean)[[3]](https://www.sofx.com/u-s-forces-board-sanctioned-oil-tanker-in-the-indian-ocean) This date aligns directly with option (D), and no conflicting information from TankerTrackers.com was found in the results.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 16, 2026. Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche previously stated that three million pages of Epstein-related files were not released for what specific reason?
(A) To protect the reputations of high-profile political figures
(B) Because the documents were improperly redacted by technical error
(C) Due to deliberative process privilege regarding prosecution decisions
(D) Because they contained internal memos from the Southern District of New York
(E) Due to personal medical files, graphic depictions of child abuse, or material jeopardizing investigations
explanation: Todd Blanche stated the pages were withheld "due to the existence of personal medical files, graphic depictions of child abuse or other material that would jeopardise investigations."
model_output:
I reviewed recent news reports and official statements from January 30, 2026, regarding Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche's announcement about the Epstein files. The Department of Justice released over 3 million pages but withheld approximately another 3 million (based on reports of a total of roughly 6 million pages under review, with about 50% not released). The specific reason Blanche provided for not releasing certain files aligns with protections under the Epstein Files Transparency Act, including personal medical files, graphic depictions of child abuse, and material that could jeopardize ongoing investigations.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 16, 2026. Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, how many children were confirmed among the nine total victims?
(A) Three children
(B) Six children
(C) Nine children
(D) Two children
(E) Five children
explanation: The article states: "residents are reeling after a mass shooting left nine people dead, including six children."
model_output:
The mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, occurred on February 10, 2026. According to available reports, a total of nine people were killed in the incident, including the 18-year-old perpetrator who died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Tumbler_Ridge_shooting). The fatalities consisted of eight people killed by the shooter (six children and two adults) plus the perpetrator[[2]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c1e98w35qyjo). The children killed were five students at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School (three 12-year-old girls, one 12-year-old boy, and one 13-year-old boy) plus the perpetrator's 11-year-old half-brother at a nearby home[[3]](https://www.cnn.com/world/live-news/tumbler-ridge-canada-shooting-02-11-26)[[4]](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/tumbler-ridge-bc-mass-shooting-grace-in-wake-of-horror-9.7090952). The perpetrator was 18 years old (an adult) and is sometimes included in the total count of nine fatalities, which aligns with the question's reference to "nine total victims." This means six children were among those nine.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 16, 2026. According to White House claims, how much will reversing the "endangerment finding" reduce costs for automobile manufacturers per car?
(A) $1.3 trillion
(B) $7.5 billion
(C) $2,400
(D) $1,760
(E) $20,000
explanation: The article states: "Reversing the finding will reduce automobile manufacturers' costs by around $2,400 (£1,760) per car, the White House claimed."
model_output:
Based on recent White House statements and related reporting as of February 16, 2026, the claimed cost reduction specifically for automobile manufacturers from reversing the EPA's "endangerment finding" (which underpins greenhouse gas emission regulations) is $2,400 per car. This figure appears in multiple sources attributing it directly to White House claims, alongside a total estimated savings of $1.3 trillion across the economy[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn0zdd7yl4vo). While some statements frame it as savings passed on to consumers via lower vehicle prices, the per-car manufacturer cost reduction is explicitly cited as $2,400[[2]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-epa-endangerment-finding-greenhouse-gases-climate-change)[[3]](https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/2026/02/president-trump-delivers-biggest-regulatory-relief-in-history). Other options like $1.3 trillion represent the aggregate savings, not the per-car figure.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn0zdd7yl4vo])
- CBS News ([https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-epa-endangerment-finding-greenhouse-gases-climate-change])
- White House ([https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/2026/02/president-trump-delivers-biggest-regulatory-relief-in-history])

explanation: The article states: "According to a recent Eurobarometer poll, more than two-thirds of Europeans (68%) feel their country is under threat."
model_output:
Based on the results from a web search for recent Eurobarometer polls as of February 16, 2026, a Flash Eurobarometer survey released in early February 2026 (around February 12-13 based on publication dates) found that 68% of Europeans believe their country is under threat[[1]](https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_403)[[2]](https://www.aa.com.tr/en/europe/large-majority-of-europeans-see-their-country-under-threat-survey/3829163)[[3]](https://en.yenisafak.com/world/eurobarometer-68-of-europeans-feel-their-country-is-under-threat-3714587). This appears to match the "recent Eurobarometer poll" referenced in the query, as it's consistently cited across multiple reports and aligns with the provided multiple-choice options. Other polls from around the same time (e.g., the EP Autumn 2025 survey) focus on different topics like general pessimism and don't mention this specific threat perception statistic.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Europeans perceive growing threat to their security and trust EU to ... (https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_403)

model_output:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>
